title: Obstacle Avoiding Robot
connections: Ultrasonic Trig → Arduino D12, Echo → D11
Arduino GND → Motor Driver GND → Battery GND
tips: Place a 100nF decoupling capacitor across motor leads to reduce electromagnetic interference (EMI) on the sensor rails.
The HC-SR04 uses high frequency sound pulses. Sound reflects poorly off soft surfaces like fabrics.

title: Line Following Robot
connections: Left IR Sensor Out → Arduino A0
Right IR Sensor Out → Arduino A1
Motors connected to Motor Driver Shield M1 and M2 terminals
tips: Adjust the sensitivity potentiometer on the IR sensor modules so they reliably distinguish black tape from light-colored floors.
Shield IR receivers from direct sunlight to prevent ambient infrared saturation.

title: Smart Auto-Opening Dustbin
tips: Mount the ultrasonic sensor in a front bezel location where hands can trigger it easily.
Protect wires from moisture inside the bin casing.

title: DHT22 LCD Weather Station
connections: DHT22 DATA → Arduino D2 (+ 10kΩ pull-up to 5V)
tips: DHT22 samples at a maximum rate of 0.5Hz (once every 2 seconds). Sampling faster will read stale or cached values.
For outdoor usage, place the sensor inside a solar radiation shield (Stevenson screen).

title: Automatic Plant Watering
connections: Moisture Sensor VCC → 5V, GND → GND, OUT → A0
tips: Soil moisture sensors corrode quickly when powered continuously. Power the sensor using an Arduino GPIO pin only when taking measurements.
Implement a timeout in code to prevent continuous pumping if the sensor fails or falls out of the pot.

title: Multi-Zone Smart Irrigation
connections: Moisture Sensors AOUT → A0, A1, A2
Relay Contacts switching 12V supply to Solenoid valves
tips: Use heavy-duty solenoid valves designed for 12V DC operation.
Check for back-EMF feedback from inductive relay coils. Snubber diodes across relays prevent circuit resets.

title: IR Sensor Smart Parking System
connections: IR Slot Sensors → D2, D3, D4
tips: IR distance sensors are affected by bright ambient lights. Use IR filter hoods if deploying in windows.
Initialize gate barriers smoothly to avoid mechanical backlash in toy servo gears.

title: Bluetooth Smart Home Controller
connections: HC-05 RXD → Arduino TXD (D1 via voltage divider)
Relay Inputs → D8, D9, D10, D11
tips: HC-05 module inputs are 3.3V logic. Use a resistor divider (2.2kΩ/1kΩ) on the RX line to reduce Arduino 5V TX signals.
Verify default baud rates (HC-05 is typically 9600 in data mode, 38400 in AT mode).

title: Voice Controlled AC Relay Box
connections: SSR Load Terminals switching AC Line
tips: AC electricity can be fatal. Use Solid State Relays (SSRs) for high switching speed and optical isolation.
Ensure voice recognition modules are trained in a room with quiet background noise profiles.

title: RFID/PIR Smart Classroom System
tips: PIR sensor detection cone is wider than expected — mount it in corners to sweep classrooms.
Use zero-cross switching relays to avoid load surge currents when incandescent light packs turn ON.
